channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump couldn't pronounce 'Assyrians.' The community is happy to be in the spotlight
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — It was Donald Trump's mispronunciation that first caught attention. “Also, we have many Asur-Asians in our room,” Trump said at a weekend rally in Prescott Valley, Arizona. “We have some incredible people in our room.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Fox News says none of its employees wrote jokes for Trump to tell at traditional campaign dinner
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fox News Channel on Friday denied Donald Trump's assertion that any of its employees wrote jokes for him to deliver this week at a New York appearance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Harris and Trump target Michigan as both parties try to shore up 'blue wall' votes
item.description: GRAND RAPIDS, Mich. (AP) — Kamala Harris and Donald Trump both sought support from Arab American voters Friday as they campaigned in Michigan, trying to lock down support in this key battleground state.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California utility shuts off power in 12 counties as 'diablo' wind spikes risk of wildfires
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A California utility shut off power in 12 counties in the northern part of the state as a major “diablo wind” — notorious in autumn for its hot, dry gusts — spiked the risk of power lines sparking a wildfire.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Condemned South Carolina inmate chooses to die by lethal injection
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A condemned South Carolina inmate chose Friday to be executed by lethal injection, instead of by firing squad or electrocution, for killing a store clerk in 1999. State prison officials had told Richard Moore this month he could choose the method for his Nov. 1 execution.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY S. COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: WATCH: Dolphins swim through neon blue bioluminescent waters off San Diego
item.description: A team from UC San Diego's Scripps Institution of Oceanography caught the beautiful sight early in the morning.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KRON4

item.title: Teacher's unlikely 'suicide' ruling called out as web sleuths dig into surveillance video
item.description: True-crime web sleuths have refocused their attention on surveillance video from the night of Ellen Greenberg's violent death in 2011, which was ruled a suicide.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Whooping cough is at a decade-high level in US
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Whooping cough is at its highest level in a decade for this time of year, U.S. health officials reported Thursday. There have been 18,506 cases of whooping cough reported so far, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention said.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, star of ‘South Pacific,’ dies at 93
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI and BOB THOMAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: The domed ballpark would have a 373,000-square-foot footprint and is expected to reach a height of 320 feet.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Google Flights adds 'Cheapest' option for travelers who just don't care how they get there
item.description: Google Flights has a new option for immediately finding the “cheapest” flight itinerary, but it may come at a slightly different cost.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Missouri 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training
item.description: Based on information gathered by NewsNation affiliate WDAF, it appears the bus driver may have been trying to teach the kids what to do in an emergency.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, NewsNation

item.title: NOAA releases 2024-25 winter weather outlook: Here's what to expect
item.description: Depending on where you live in the U.S., this winter could bring a mix of snow and rain or unseasonably warm weather, according to climate experts.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the Los Angeles Catholic Church's $880M settlement with sexual abuse victims
item.description: The Archdiocese of Los Angeles has agreed to pay $880 million to hundreds of victims of clergy sexual abuse dating back decades.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: BRADENTON BEACH, Fla. (AP) — When a hurricane sets its sights on Florida, storm-weary residents may think of catastrophic wind, hammering rain and dangerous storm surge. Mounds of sand swallowing their homes? Not so much.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, KTLA 5

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, KRON4

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of LA to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KRON4

item.title: Dollar General employee finds puppies dumped in trash amid cold Arkansas weather
item.description: Three puppies, less than a week old, were abandoned this week inside of a Dollar General Market dumpster, according to Arkansas authorities.
item.dc:creator: Elena Ramirez, ABC 4

item.title: Navy finds wreckage of fighter jet that crashed during training in Washington state
item.description: The U.S. Navy located the wreckage Wednesday of a fighter jet that crashed in Washington state during a routine training flight in mountainous terrain. The two crew members onboard remain missing.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pizza Hut unveils new restaurant — but there's a catch
item.description: Pizza Hut is unveiling a new restaurant — but there's a catch: There is only one location and it will only be open for two days. Also, you can only get one size of pizza. In honor of National Pizza Month, the chain is introducing the Personal Pan Pizza Hut.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KTLA 5

item.title: Minnesota school district bans officer from teaching after he reenacted George Floyd's murder
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A Minnesota school district has banned a police officer from working as a substitute teacher after a series of “racially harmful” actions that officials say included putting a student on the ground for a reenactment of the 2020 murder of George Floyd by a Minneapolis officer.
item.dc:creator: BY STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Listeria recall grows to 12 million pounds of meat and poultry, some of it sent to US schools
item.description: A nationwide recall of meat and poultry products potentially contaminated with listeria has expanded to nearly 12 million pounds and now includes ready-to-eat meals sent to U.S. schools, restaurants and major retailers, federal officials said.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Delphi murders: State wants to keep widely circulated composite sketches out of trial
item.description: CARROLL COUNTY, Ind. – The state doesn’t want a pair of composite sketches that were highly publicized early in the investigation to be part of the Delphi murders trial.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, WGN 9

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, WGN 9

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, Fox 5

item.title: Tom Donohue, former longtime Chamber of Commerce CEO, dies
item.description: Tom Donohue, who led the U.S. Chamber of Commerce for more than two decades before retiring in 2021, has died, the business lobbying giant announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, WGN 9

item.title: Lilly Ledbetter, equal pay trailblazer, dies at 86
item.description: Lilly Ledbetter, an activist for equal pay whose legal fight against her employer paved the way for the Fair Pay Act, has died. She was 86.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9
